methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
IQM Quantum Computers is a pre-IPO quantum computing company that has raised €600M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across the quantum computing sector: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, Quantinuum) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 7 same-sector peers exist globally, and filtering for stage alignment and valuation tier yields fewer than the three datapoints required for robust median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the extreme heterogeneity in quantum computing business models, technology approaches, and commercialization timelines across the comp set.

Without triangulation via peer multiples, valuation of IQM requires alternative anchors: cost-of-capital frameworks, precedent transaction analysis within quantum hardware, or venture capital method backed to specific revenue or deployment milestones. The €600M+ raised to date signals investor conviction but does not mechanically determine fair value. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation versus competing modalities (superconducting, ion trap, neutral atom), path to revenue generation, and the timing and terms of potential exit events. Any valuation conclusion must remain conditional on clarification of IQM's technical standing relative to the named peer set and explicit assumptions about medium-term commercialization success.
